evals.prompt: Initialize rules for this established C# repository, but inspect its AGENTS.md and get my approval before changing anything.
evals.expected_output: A read-only research report and explicit approval gate; no repository changes before approval.
assertions: The response reads the existing instruction hierarchy before proposing rules.
The response does not use rules init --apply before explicit approval.
The proposed checks use argv arrays rather than shell strings.

evals.prompt: I cloned MyDimerco on a new computer. Restore its existing LLM Wiki rules into this checkout; don't make me find the old JSON or remember multiple commands.
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies the repository, finds the central policy, and materializes and verifies local AGENTS.md/.ai-policy artifacts through the single project workflow.
assertions: The response checks the central project-policy store before researching new rules.
The response does not ask the user to manually locate or copy a proposal JSON file.
The response restores repository-local AGENTS.md and .ai-policy artifacts without committing them.

evals.prompt: Update this project's AI rules based on the current architecture and keep LLM Wiki as the reusable source of truth.
evals.expected_output: The skill compares central and local state, researches the delta, requests approval for policy content changes, then updates central and local artifacts together.
assertions: The response starts with the single project state check rather than asking the user to choose init, sync, or check.
The response requests approval for the complete changed policy before writing.
The response updates both the central vault policy and repository-local enforcement artifacts and runs preflight.

evals.prompt: Set up rules for this Python repository. It has pytest but no linter, formatter, or type checker. Recommend current tools, but do not change dependencies until I approve the complete proposal.
evals.expected_output: The skill reports deterministic tooling gaps, verifies candidates with official sources, and stops before dependency or policy writes.
assertions: The response distinguishes detected tools from missing capabilities.
The response cites official tool documentation and lists exact proposed file changes and argv checks.
The response does not reject Ruff or a type checker solely because adding development tooling requires approval.
The response does not install tools, change dependency files, or apply policy before explicit approval.
